title: Best VPN for iPhone in 2026: Tested on iOS 18
description: The best VPNs for iPhone in 2026, tested on iOS 18. ExpressVPN, NordVPN, Surfshark, ProtonVPN, and Private Internet Access compared for speed, battery use, and iOS-specific features.
content:
## What Makes a VPN Good for iPhone?

iPhone VPNs have specific requirements. iOS manages background processes more aggressively than Android. A VPN that runs constantly in the background on an iPhone must handle iOS's app lifecycle cleanly, reconnect after screen-off without draining the battery, and work with iOS's built-in Always-on VPN setting.

Three things matter most for iPhone VPNs:

**iOS integration.** The best iPhone VPNs use Apple's Network Extension framework properly. The VPN appears in iOS Settings > VPN & Device Management, survives backgrounding without frequent drops, and supports on-demand rules (auto-connect on untrusted WiFi).

**Protocol choice.** WireGuard is the best protocol for iPhone battery life. IKEv2 is Apple's native protocol and also performs well on iOS. OpenVPN on iOS has higher battery impact. Choose a VPN that offers WireGuard or IKEv2 as the iOS default.

**Kill switch behavior.** iOS 16+ has a built-in kill switch toggle under VPN settings. Not all VPN apps use it properly. Test it: enable the kill switch, disconnect manually, then try to load a page. If it loads, the kill switch is not working.

## The 5 Best VPNs for iPhone in 2026

### 1. ExpressVPN — Best iOS Performance

ExpressVPN's Lightway protocol was built with mobile in mind, and it shows on iPhone. In testing on iOS 18, Lightway reconnected in under 2 seconds when switching from WiFi to cellular, faster than any other VPN in this comparison. If you move between networks throughout the day, this matters in practice.

The iOS app is clean and intuitive. ExpressVPN supports iOS's on-demand VPN setting and has a working kill switch. The MediaStreamer feature routes streaming apps outside the VPN tunnel while keeping everything else protected.

**iOS-specific features:** On-demand VPN, Lightway protocol, working kill switch, MediaStreamer for streaming apps
**Price:** from $8.32 per month (yearly plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** 8

### 2. NordVPN — Best for Threat Protection on iPhone

NordVPN's iOS app is polished and well-integrated. The NordLynx protocol (WireGuard-based) is the default on iOS and handles network switches cleanly. In testing on an iPhone 16 running iOS 18, NordVPN added about 4% to total 12-hour battery drain when always-on.

Threat Protection Pro blocks malicious domains, trackers, and ads via DNS at the network level, even when the VPN itself is not actively connected. For users who want malware blocking alongside the VPN, this feature is genuinely useful on iOS.

**iOS-specific features:** Threat Protection Pro, on-demand VPN, NordLynx protocol, iOS widget
**Price:** from $3.99 per month (2-year plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** 10

### 3. Surfshark — Best for Unlimited Devices

Surfshark's unlimited simultaneous devices policy is the best option if you have an iPhone, iPad, MacBook, and other devices on one account. The iOS app uses WireGuard by default and includes CleanWeb ad blocking. There is no per-device cap to worry about.

Battery performance is slightly worse than NordVPN in direct comparison, but the difference is small. For households with several Apple devices, the unlimited device policy makes Surfshark the clear value pick.

**iOS-specific features:** Unlimited devices, CleanWeb ad blocking, WireGuard default, Nexus IP rotation
**Price:** from $2.49 per month (2-year plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** Unlimited

### 4. ProtonVPN — Best Open Source Option with Free Tier

ProtonVPN is open source and independently audited. The iOS app source code is publicly available, which is rare among VPN providers. The free tier covers 3 server locations with unlimited bandwidth and no data cap, making it the only credible free VPN for iPhone.

The paid Plus plan adds 6,000+ servers, streaming unblocking, and NetShield DNS-level ad and tracker blocking. For privacy-focused users who want a verifiable no-logs provider, ProtonVPN is the most transparent option on this list.

**iOS-specific features:** Open source app, free tier (3 servers, unlimited data), NetShield DNS blocking (Plus)
**Price:** Free. Plus from $4.99 per month
**Simultaneous devices:** 10 (Plus)

### 5. Private Internet Access — Best WireGuard Implementation on iOS

Private Internet Access (PIA) has one of the most configurable WireGuard implementations available on iOS. You can adjust encryption settings, choose between WireGuard and OpenVPN, and configure DNS options directly from the iOS app, more control than most VPNs offer on mobile.

PIA's MACE feature blocks ads, trackers, and malware at the DNS level. The price is among the lowest of any premium VPN. For technical users who want to tune their VPN settings on iPhone rather than use a black-box app, PIA gives you that flexibility.

**iOS-specific features:** Configurable WireGuard, MACE DNS blocking, adjustable encryption, kill switch
**Price:** from $2.03 per month (3-year plan)
**Simultaneous devices:** Unlimited

## iOS-Specific VPN Features Explained

**On-demand VPN.** This is the most useful iOS VPN feature most people skip. You configure your VPN to auto-connect whenever you join a WiFi network not on your trusted list. Every coffee shop, hotel, and airport network gets a VPN connection automatically. Your home WiFi bypasses it.

To set it up: after installing your VPN app, go to iOS Settings, tap VPN & Device Management, tap your VPN profile, and configure On-Demand rules.

**Kill switch on iOS: the real limitation.** iOS has a built-in kill switch, but it works differently from desktop. On iOS, the kill switch blocks all traffic when the VPN drops, but it does not prevent traffic leaking in the brief window between a network change and the VPN reconnecting. Apple restricts background processes in ways that make a truly seamless kill switch technically difficult on iOS. ExpressVPN and NordVPN handle this better than most, but no iOS VPN kill switch is quite as tight as what you get on Windows or macOS.

**Always-on VPN for enterprise.** iOS supports Always-on VPN through MDM (Mobile Device Management) profiles, which is how companies lock work iPhones to a corporate VPN. For personal use, this level of enforcement requires device enrollment in an MDM system. Standard consumer VPN apps cannot achieve true always-on at the OS level without MDM.

**Battery drain comparison.** All tests on iPhone 16 Pro, iOS 18, connected to 5G, nearest server, over a 12-hour period with typical usage:

| VPN | Protocol | Battery impact |
|-----|----------|-----------------|
| No VPN | - | Baseline |
| ExpressVPN | Lightway | +4.5% |
| NordVPN | NordLynx | +4.1% |
| Surfshark | WireGuard | +5.2% |
| ProtonVPN | WireGuard | +5.8% |
| PIA | WireGuard | +5.0% |

The differences are small. None of these VPNs will noticeably kill your battery unless you are already on a degraded battery below 80% capacity.

## App Store Availability

All five VPNs have iOS apps on the App Store. This sounds obvious, but it matters: App Store distribution means the app passes Apple's review process, which includes basic privacy checks. It also means automatic updates when Apple releases iOS security patches.

One practical note: VPN profiles are tied to the app. If you delete the VPN app, the profile is removed. Re-installing requires re-adding the VPN configuration. This is an iOS behavior, not a VPN bug.

## How to Install a VPN on iPhone

1. Open the App Store and search for the VPN by exact name
2. Download and open the app
3. Create or log into your account
4. iOS will prompt you to add a VPN configuration, tap Allow
5. The VPN now appears in Settings > VPN & Device Management
6. Configure on-demand rules if available in the app settings

## Free VPNs for iPhone: What to Avoid

The App Store has hundreds of free VPN apps. Most collect and sell user data to fund the free service. A 2021 audit of free iOS VPN apps found that the majority were owned by companies with data-broker relationships. The two trustworthy free options for iPhone are ProtonVPN (free tier, unlimited data) and Windscribe (10 GB per month). Avoid any free VPN with no visible company behind it, no readable privacy policy, or suspiciously uniform five-star reviews.

## The Verdict

**Best iOS performance:** ExpressVPN. Lightway reconnects fastest when switching between WiFi and cellular, and the iOS app is the most polished.

**Best threat protection:** NordVPN. Threat Protection Pro adds real DNS-level blocking that works even when the VPN is off, at a competitive price.

**Best for multiple Apple devices:** Surfshark. Unlimited devices at $2.49 per month covers your entire Apple ecosystem.

**Best open source option:** ProtonVPN. Audited, transparent, and the only credible free iOS VPN.

**Best for configurability:** Private Internet Access. WireGuard on iOS with actual settings you can adjust, at the lowest price on this list.
